user.title: Location
user.description: Enter the building location. Tortoise-UFH uses latitude and longitude for weather compensation and solar-gain feedforward.

rooms.title: Define Rooms
rooms.description: Add the rooms (zones) that Tortoise-UFH should control. Each room runs its own independent closed-loop controller. Add at least one room.

data.has_fast_source: This room has a fast auxiliary source

data_description.fast_source_window_start: Outside this window the room's split/heater will not engage (quiet hours); a running unit stops only after its minimum run time. The window may cross midnight (e.g. 07:00–22:00 or 22:00–07:00). Leave both empty to always allow. Applies only to rooms with a fast source.
data_description.fast_source_window_end: Outside this window the room's split/heater will not engage (quiet hours); a running unit stops only after its minimum run time. The window may cross midnight (e.g. 07:00–22:00 or 22:00–07:00). Leave both empty to always allow. Applies only to rooms with a fast source.

entities.title: Entity Mapping: {room_name}
entities.description: Select the Home Assistant entities for {room_name}. Tortoise-UFH validates units only (°C, %, W) and stays hardware-agnostic. A room may have several UFH loops, so valve, supply, and return accept multiple entities. Global entities are shown once, on the first room.

data.entity_valves: UFH valve actuators — number or valve entities (one per loop)
data.entity_supply: Supply water temperature sensors (one per loop)
data.entity_return: Return water temperature sensors (one per loop)
data.entity_mode: Global mode entity (heating / transitional / cooling / off)

algorithm.title: Algorithm Parameters
algorithm.description: Configure the whole-home setpoint and the PI + trend controller gains. Defaults are sensible for a high-thermal-mass UFH house; leave them unless you know what you are tuning.

data.hp_flicker_band_k: Force cooling start: target deadband (K, global)
data.hp_flicker_stuck_minutes: Force cooling start: delay before forcing a start (min, global)
data.hp_flicker_min_off_minutes: Force cooling start: min time between forced starts (min, global)
data.hp_flicker_max_starts_per_h: Force cooling start: max forced starts per hour (global)
data.flow_epsilon_k: Flow watchdog: minimum ΔT counting as flow (K)

confirm.title: Confirm Setup
confirm.description: Tortoise-UFH will be set up with {num_rooms} room(s) at location ({latitude}, {longitude}), home setpoint {home_setpoint} °C. Rooms start in the Off state — nothing is written to your hardware until you switch a room to Live. Confirm to complete setup.

error.invalid_location: Invalid home location — latitude must be between -90 and 90 and longitude between -180 and 180
error.duplicate_room_name: A room with this name already exists
error.duplicate_room_slug: A room whose name differs only in case or spacing already exists (they would collide in the registry)
error.valve_required: At least one valve actuator must be selected
error.humidity_required: A humidity sensor is required for rooms participating in floor cooling
error.entity_not_found: Entity not found in Home Assistant
error.invalid_unit: Entity has an incorrect unit of measurement
error.invalid_device_class: Entity has an incorrect device class
error.entity_unavailable: Entity is currently unavailable — it may come online later
error.valve_no_set_position: This valve entity does not support setting a position (SET_POSITION). Pick a position-capable valve or a number entity.
error.quiet_window_invalid: Set both window times (start and end, different from each other) or neither.

abort.already_configured: Tortoise-UFH is already configured for this location

init.title: Tortoise-UFH
init.description: Manage rooms or adjust control and tuning settings.

settings.title: Control & Tuning
settings.description: Set each room's control state — off (excluded from control, nothing is written to the hardware) or live (drives its valves and fast source) — and adjust the advanced controller gains. The panel and the per-room control-state select entities are the primary surface; this form is an emergency / automation-free fallback.

data.hp_flicker_band_k: Force cooling start: target deadband (K, global)
data.hp_flicker_stuck_minutes: Force cooling start: delay before forcing a start (min, global)
data.hp_flicker_min_off_minutes: Force cooling start: min time between forced starts (min, global)
data.hp_flicker_max_starts_per_h: Force cooling start: max forced starts per hour (global)
data.flow_epsilon_k: Flow watchdog: minimum ΔT counting as flow (K)

data_description.entity_global_supply: Optional probe on the manifold supply bar, before the loop valves. It feeds the hydraulic flow watchdog's circulation gate: when it clearly reads source-side of the mean room temperature, circulation is considered proven even while every per-loop probe sits post-valve. Note: during DHW/defrost (pump-available entity off) the gate ignores this probe.

add_room.title: Add a Room
add_room.description: Define a new room. After its attributes you will map its Home Assistant source entities. Each room runs its own independent closed-loop controller.

data.has_fast_source: This room has a fast auxiliary source

data_description.fast_source_window_start: Outside this window the room's split/heater will not engage (quiet hours); a running unit stops only after its minimum run time. The window may cross midnight (e.g. 07:00–22:00 or 22:00–07:00). Leave both empty to always allow. Applies only to rooms with a fast source.
data_description.fast_source_window_end: Outside this window the room's split/heater will not engage (quiet hours); a running unit stops only after its minimum run time. The window may cross midnight (e.g. 07:00–22:00 or 22:00–07:00). Leave both empty to always allow. Applies only to rooms with a fast source.

edit_room.title: Edit a Room
edit_room.description: Pick the room to edit. Its name cannot be changed (rename = remove + add).

edit_room_attrs.title: Edit Room: {room_name}
edit_room_attrs.description: Update the attributes for {room_name}, then its entity mapping. The room name is immutable.

data.has_fast_source: This room has a fast auxiliary source

data_description.fast_source_window_start: Outside this window the room's split/heater will not engage (quiet hours); a running unit stops only after its minimum run time. The window may cross midnight (e.g. 07:00–22:00 or 22:00–07:00). Leave both empty to always allow. Applies only to rooms with a fast source.
data_description.fast_source_window_end: Outside this window the room's split/heater will not engage (quiet hours); a running unit stops only after its minimum run time. The window may cross midnight (e.g. 07:00–22:00 or 22:00–07:00). Leave both empty to always allow. Applies only to rooms with a fast source.

remove_room.title: Remove a Room
remove_room.description: Pick the room to remove. Its diagnostic entities are deleted and its per-room settings are pruned. This cannot be undone.

room_entities.title: Entity Mapping: {room_name}
room_entities.description: Select the Home Assistant entities for {room_name}. Tortoise-UFH validates units only (°C, %, W) and stays hardware-agnostic. A room may have several UFH loops, so valve, supply, and return accept multiple entities.

data.entity_valves: UFH valve actuators — number or valve entities (one per loop)
data.entity_supply: Supply water temperature sensors (one per loop)
data.entity_return: Return water temperature sensors (one per loop)

heat_pump.title: Heat pump (optional link)
heat_pump.description: All fields are optional — leave everything empty to keep Tortoise away from the pump. Direction sync always preserves the pump's “+DHW” flag; the external DHW automation stays its owner. Example HeishaMon-style entities: select.heat_pump_mode, number.z1_cool_request_temp.

data.entity_hp_return_temp: Return/inlet water temperature sensor (optional, for flicker)

data_description.entity_hp_mode: HeishaMon-style select with options like “Heat only” / “Cool only” / “Heat+DHW”. Tortoise writes only the direction and never removes the DHW flag.
data_description.entity_hp_active: Off while the pump does DHW/defrost — the room controllers then freeze their integrators.
data_description.entity_hp_return_temp: The pump inlet/return water probe. Main flicker signal: the return parks near cooling setpoint + 3 K through long idles. Panasonic example: sensor.panasonic_heat_pump_main_main_inlet_temp.
data_description.entity_hp_outlet_temp: The pump outlet/supply water probe. Diagnostic only in this version — shown on the Heat pump tab; no logic uses it yet (reserved for a future supply-side condensation guard).
data_description.entity_hp_compressor_freq: Compressor frequency in Hz (0 = off). Used to detect the pump idling in its deadband. Panasonic example: sensor.panasonic_heat_pump_main_compressor_freq.

error.duplicate_room_name: A room with this name already exists
error.duplicate_room_slug: A room whose name differs only in case or spacing already exists (they would collide in the registry)
error.valve_required: At least one valve actuator must be selected
error.humidity_required: A humidity sensor is required for rooms participating in floor cooling
error.entity_not_found: Entity not found in Home Assistant
error.invalid_unit: Entity has an incorrect unit of measurement
error.invalid_device_class: Entity has an incorrect device class
error.entity_unavailable: Entity is currently unavailable — it may come online later
error.valve_no_set_position: This valve entity does not support setting a position (SET_POSITION). Pick a position-capable valve or a number entity.
error.quiet_window_invalid: Set both window times (start and end, different from each other) or neither.

abort.no_rooms: There are no rooms to manage
abort.cannot_remove_last_room: Cannot remove the last room — at least one room is required

set_home_temperature.name: Set home temperature
set_home_temperature.description: Set the global home target temperature in degrees Celsius. Per-room setpoints are derived as home temperature plus each room's offset.

temperature.name: Temperature
temperature.description: Global home target temperature in degrees Celsius.

set_room_offset.name: Set room offset
set_room_offset.description: Set the temperature offset for a single room in kelvin. The room setpoint equals the global home temperature plus this offset.

room.name: Room
room.description: Identifier of the room to adjust.

offset.name: Offset
offset.description: Temperature offset for the room in kelvin (relative to home).

set_mode.name: Set mode
set_mode.description: Set the global operating mode of the Tortoise-UFH system.

mode.name: Mode
mode.description: Operating mode of the system (heating / transitional / cooling / off).

test_actuation.name: Test actuation
test_actuation.description: Run a manual actuation self-test for one room: the valve is deliberately driven to 100 % for the given duration and the loop's hydraulic response (supply/return probes) decides pass or fail. Meant to be run after maintenance or a power event — never scheduled. Requires the room to be LIVE with water probes configured; safety rules (overheat, condensation) abort the test.

room.name: Room
room.description: Identifier of the room to test.

cancel.name: Cancel
cancel.description: Cancel the room's running self-test instead of starting one.
